content:
- Completed the HTTPS certificate bootstrap bug fix: `ListenerTLSConfig.ServerTLSConfig()` now loads the server key pair, and `Run(...)` now serves with `ListenAndServeTLS("", "")` so runtime no longer owns cert file wiring.
- Added `verifyservice/runtime_test.go` with two vertical TDD slices: a direct TLS-config test and a FIFO-backed runtime regression test that proves startup still works after the cert files are deleted once preload has happened.
- Validation completed: `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./verifyservice -count=1`, `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all pass. A broader `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./...` still has existing environment-dependent failures outside this task.
- Updated the task file and plan checkboxes, set `<passes>true</passes>`, and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`. Next was commit+push, and then stop.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and read the choose-task rules plus previous progress.
- Compared the two remaining unblocked failing high-priority verify HTTP bugs against the actual Go verifyservice code under cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice.
- Chose the unbounded request-body bug as the next task because POST /jobs and POST /stop decode directly from r.Body with no size limit, making it the more immediate remotely-triggerable DoS boundary than completed-job retention, which is partially throttled by the single-active-job model.
- Next after quit: work the request-body limit bug with Red-Green TDD, then run make check, make test, and make lint.

- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-http-request-body-size-is-unbounded.md.
- Selection is complete and respects bug-first plus priority rules: this is the top unblocked failing high-priority bug based on the current verifyservice attack surface.
- Next after quit: implement body-size limiting with strict TDD and re-run make check, make test, and make lint.

- Read Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the `improve-code-boundaries` plus `tdd` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-http-request-body-size-is-unbounded.md` and that the intended next step is strict TDD on verifyservice request body limits.
- Next after quitting immediately due to context limit: read the task file, determine whether it says `TO BE VERIFIED` or `NOW EXECUTE`, then either create/update the plan or execute it.

- Read the active bug task, the verifyservice request handlers/tests, and the repo's existing task-plan conventions.
- Created `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-http-request-body-size-is-unbounded_plans/2026-04-19-verify-http-request-body-limit-plan.md`, linked it from the task markdown, and finalized the plan marker to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- The plan centers the fix on the shared `decodeJSONBody` boundary so `POST /jobs` and `POST /stop` get one canonical size limit, explicit `413` handling, and strict red-green slices without adding config sprawl.
- Next after quitting immediately due to context limit: execute the plan with vertical TDD, starting with one failing `POST /jobs` oversized-body integration test, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` once the bug is fixed.

- Read previous Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill docs.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-http-request-body-size-is-unbounded.md` and prior progress says a plan was already finalized to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: read the task markdown and its linked plan, then execute the plan with strict vertical TDD if the design still holds.

- Read the active bug task and its linked plan; plan is still marked `NOW EXECUTE` and the shared decode boundary remains the right fix shape.
- Next: inspect `verifyservice/service.go` plus `verifyservice/http_test.go`, then add the first RED integration test for oversized `POST /jobs` before any implementation change.

- Inspected `verifyservice/service.go` and confirmed both `POST /jobs` and `POST /stop` currently feed unbounded `r.Body` into the same `decodeJSONBody` helper.
- Next: add the first RED integration test in `verifyservice/http_test.go` for oversized `POST /jobs`, then run only the targeted package test to prove the failure before touching implementation.

- Added the first RED integration test for oversized `POST /jobs` in `verifyservice/http_test.go`.
- Initial test invocation failed because the Go module root is `cockroachdb_molt/molt`, not the repo root; rerunning the targeted red test there now.
- Next: inspect the actual failing behavior, then implement the smallest shared decode-boundary fix to turn that one test green.

- Implemented the smallest shared-boundary fix in `verifyservice/service.go`: `decodeJSONBody` now wraps the body with `http.MaxBytesReader`, normalizes `*http.MaxBytesError` to a sentinel error, and both write handlers route decode failures through one status-mapping helper.
- Next: rerun the first targeted test to confirm the tracer bullet is GREEN before checking whether `POST /stop` still needs its own red slice.

- The first tracer-bullet test is green. The shared bounded decoder now also covers `POST /stop`, so slice 2 manual verification is by shared code path: the bug no longer holds there and no second red test is needed.
- Next: run the full `verifyservice` package tests, then do one final code-boundary pass before repository-wide `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Focused package validation exposed a second boundary issue: trailing-document validation returned a raw unknown-field decode error on the second JSON document.
- Fixed `decodeJSONBody` so the first decode still enforces unknown fields, while the follow-up decode reads into `any` and now reliably returns `request body must contain exactly one JSON object` for extra documents.
- Next: rerun the full `verifyservice` package tests, then proceed to repo-wide `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` if the package is clean.

- `go test ./verifyservice` now passes after the shared decoder fixes.
- Running `gofmt`, checking the diff for one-helper ownership of the request boundary, and then moving into the required repository validation lanes.

- `make check` passed cleanly from repo root.
- Next: finish the required validation with `make lint` and `make test`, then update the Ralph task/plan metadata only if both lanes stay green.

- `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed cleanly.
- Next: update the active bug task and plan checkboxes/status, set `<passes>true</passes>`, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then commit and push all tracked Ralph/task/code changes.

- Updated the active bug task and its execution plan: status completed, passes true, and all relevant checkboxes are now marked.
- Running `.ralph/task_switch.sh` now as required before the final commit/push flow.
